title: abronia ammophila
intro: Abronia ammophila, also known as sand verbena, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Nyctaginaceae family. It is a perennial herb with fragrant, pink or white flowers that are native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Abronia ammophila include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its herbal benefits.
